name: ScrapingAnt Web Scraping API
what_it_does: Web scraping API providing headless browser rendering with anti-bot bypass, rotating proxies, and JavaScript execution — enabling agents to reliably scrape JavaScript-heavy websites and handle bot protection without managing browser infrastructure.
use_cases: Scraping JavaScript-rendered websites that require browser execution via simple API
Bypassing bot protection (Cloudflare, Datadome) on target websites for data collection
Extracting structured data from web pages with custom JavaScript execution
Rotating proxy requests through residential IPs for geo-targeted scraping
Building web data collection pipelines without managing headless browser infrastructure
not_for: Legal compliance — scraping must respect robots.txt and terms of service
Real-time streaming data collection (ScrapingAnt is request-based)
Websites where direct API access is available (prefer official APIs)
best_when: An agent needs to reliably scrape JavaScript-heavy websites with bot protection bypass, without managing browser infrastructure or proxy rotation.
avoid_when: Your target sites have official APIs, you need real-time streaming, or the sites you need to scrape prohibit automated access.

auth.notes: API key from ScrapingAnt dashboard. Passed as query parameter or header. Account-scoped access. Credit-based usage — different requests consume different credit amounts. Key management in dashboard.

pricing.paid_tiers: Starter from $19/month for 100K credits; Business from $99; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Credit-based pricing — headless browser requests cost more than non-JS requests. Generous free tier for development. Competitive pricing vs. BrightData. Good for moderate-scale scraping.

performance.rate_limits: Concurrent request limits per plan; headless browser requests slower

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Simple REST API with clear parameters for browser options, proxy type, and JavaScript execution. SDK for Python and JS. Credit usage per request visible. Error responses indicate bot detection or network failures.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Same URL request returns current page state — not idempotent if page content changes. Retry of failed requests is safe — credits only consumed on successful response.
known_agent_gotchas: Credits consumed per request — monitor usage to avoid unexpected overages
Headless browser requests significantly slower (3-15s) than simple HTTP requests
Bot bypass not guaranteed — some sophisticated anti-bot systems may still block
JavaScript execution results returned in body — parse rendered HTML appropriately

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. API key auth. GDPR compliant. Note: web scraping legality varies by jurisdiction and target site TOS — users responsible for compliance.
